name: Presidency / Operation Vulindlela
key_interests: Cross-cutting structural reform coordination. Champions energy, logistics, water, digital, and visa reform. Directly accountable to the Presidency — the most reform-committed node in government.

name: National Treasury
key_interests: Fiscal consolidation, debt sustainability, structural reforms that improve revenue without expanding contingent liabilities. Cautious on SOE bailouts; supportive of private-sector led reforms.

name: COSATU
key_interests: ANC alliance partner. Protects public-sector jobs, opposes privatisation, supports state-led energy transition. Internally divided on Eskom unbundling — NUMSA breakaway weakened its leverage.

name: South African Reserve Bank
key_interests: Monetary stability, financial system integrity, prudential oversight. Supportive of fintech entry insofar as it doesn't destabilise the payment system. Watches fiscal reform closely for inflation implications.

name: Business Unity South Africa (BUSA)
key_interests: Cross-cutting reform — energy security, logistics efficiency, regulatory certainty, labour market flexibility. Key NEDLAC interlocutor. Consistently advocates structural reform across all five packages.

name: Eskom
key_interests: Maintaining integrated utility structure, managing R400bn+ debt, labour relations with NUM/NUMSA. Unbundling of distribution arm contested internally. Reform champions exist but face institutional inertia.

name: DTIC (Dept. of Trade, Industry & Competition)
key_interests: Industrial policy, localisation requirements, SEZs, BBBEE compliance. Supportive of logistics reform insofar as it reduces input costs for manufacturers; ambivalent about full liberalisation.

name: NERSA
key_interests: Maintaining regulatory jurisdiction over electricity tariffs and licensing. Historically under-resourced; slow to approve IPP licences. Reform proposals reducing NERSA's gatekeeping role meet institutional resistance.

name: Transnet
key_interests: Port and rail monopoly protection, capital investment backlog, labour costs. State capture fallout still being addressed. Concessioning of Durban port and freight rail corridors contested internally.

name: Business Leadership South Africa (BLSA)
key_interests: CEO-level advocacy body for large corporates. Runs the CEO Initiative on inclusive growth. Publicly committed to Operation Vulindlela reform agenda across energy, logistics, and digital.

name: Minerals Council South Africa
key_interests: Energy costs and reliability, rail and port access for export corridors, regulatory certainty, royalty regime. Own-generation licences vital — strongly supportive of energy liberalisation.

name: Competition Commission
key_interests: Market concentration across freight, telecoms, financial services, food. Strong institutional capacity; has taken on major cases in banking, grocery, and telecoms. Actively supportive of opening concentrated markets.

name: National Union of Mineworkers (NUM)
key_interests: Mining employment, labour protections, just transition pace, energy sector jobs. Supportive of renewable energy in principle but demands job guarantees in coal regions before reform accelerates.

name: NUMSA
key_interests: Metalworkers with large Eskom and manufacturing membership. Strongly opposes energy unbundling, IPP model, and concessioning. Major disruptor of Eskom operations; broke from COSATU in 2014.

name: DMRE (Dept. of Mineral Resources & Energy)
key_interests: Regulatory gatekeeping over generation and mining. Historically resistant to unbundling Eskom and liberalising IPP procurement. Under pressure from Operation Vulindlela to accelerate REIPPP.

name: ICASA
key_interests: Spectrum licensing, competition in telecoms, consumer protection. Long-delayed spectrum auction completed 2022. Open to further liberalisation but resource-constrained and slow-moving.

name: Renewable Energy IPP Developers
key_interests: REIPPP Bid Window pipeline, wheeling tariffs, grid access, offtake certainty from Eskom and municipalities. Strongest private-sector champions of energy reform. Backed by international capital.

name: SAFTU
key_interests: Anti-austerity, nationalisation, worker ownership. Most vocal opponent of the reform agenda; mobilises street-level and legal pressure against privatisation and structural adjustment.

name: US / AGOA Trade Relations
key_interests: Preferential trade access under AGOA, investment climate, IP protection, digital trade rules. AGOA renewal uncertainty creates pressure on SA reform trajectory and geopolitical alignment.

name: World Bank Group
key_interests: Structural reform technical assistance and financing. Active in energy transition (JETP), logistics diagnostics, and water sector. Provides Development Policy Loans conditional on reform milestones.

name: Dept. of Public Enterprises (DPE)
key_interests: SOE oversight — Transnet, Eskom unbundling. Mandate includes restructuring SOEs while protecting jobs. Caught between National Treasury's reform pressure and labour's resistance.

name: DCDT (Dept. of Communications & Digital Tech)
key_interests: Spectrum allocation, broadband rollout, digital economy ambitions, SA Connect broadband plan. Generally supportive of competition in telecoms and digital infrastructure expansion.

name: PRASA
key_interests: Commuter rail rehabilitation, rolling stock procurement, urban mobility. Governance crises have impeded reform. Urban mobility reform depends critically on PRASA turnaround.

name: OUTA
key_interests: Fiscal accountability, SOE governance, infrastructure pricing. Campaigns against toll roads, Eskom tariff increases, and wasteful public spending. Litigation-focused and media-savvy.

name: IMF
key_interests: Fiscal sustainability, SOE contingent liabilities, labour market flexibility, current account dynamics. Article IV consultations consistently highlight structural reform gaps. No programme engagement but strong reputational influence.

name: European Union / JETP Partners
key_interests: Just Energy Transition Partnership (JETP) — €8.5bn committed for coal transition. Conditions include accelerating renewable rollout and coal community support. CBAM creates export pressure.

name: Financial Sector Conduct Authority (FSCA)
key_interests: Consumer protection in financial markets, retirement fund reform, financial inclusion. Twin Peaks regulation framework. Broadly supportive of fintech entry and inclusion reforms.

name: Telkom
key_interests: Fibre network monetisation, spectrum position, competitive response to MTN/Vodacom. Partial privatisation underway. Ambivalent about further telecoms liberalisation that reduces its incumbent advantage.

name: Fintech & Payments Sector
key_interests: Open banking, Payment Systems Act reform, lower interchange fees, financial inclusion products. Growing ecosystem backed by international capital. Strongest champions of financial sector reform.

name: AgriSA / Commercial Agriculture
key_interests: Water allocation reform, irrigation infrastructure, logistics for export corridors, land tenure security. Water reform is double-edged — supports efficiency gains but fears redistribution implications.

name: Section27
key_interests: Right to health, water, and social services. Supportive of water infrastructure investment but watchful over privatisation risks. Litigates against state failure to deliver basic services.

name: Equal Education
key_interests: School infrastructure, electrification, broadband in education. Supportive of energy and digital connectivity reforms insofar as they benefit public schools and underserved communities.

name: African Development Bank
key_interests: Infrastructure financing — energy, transport, water. Committed capital to REIPPP and logistics corridor upgrades. Regional integration lens; supports intra-African connectivity reforms.

name: Portfolio Committee on Energy (Parliament)
key_interests: Legislative oversight of NERSA and DMRE. Members span ANC, DA, EFF with divergent views. Energy regulation bills and REIPPP changes require committee scrutiny and approval.

name: Portfolio Committee on Transport (Parliament)
key_interests: Transnet reform, PRASA accountability, concessioning oversight. Mixed constituency interests — labour and business both lobby intensely. Concessioning bills and port reforms must pass here.

name: Public Affairs Research Institute (PARI)
key_interests: State capacity, institutional reform, governance quality. Provides analytical firepower for evidence-based reform. No direct political constituency but influential in policy circles.

name: SERI (Socio-Economic Rights Institute)
key_interests: Land rights, housing, informal settlement services, water access. Sceptical of market-led reform; monitors distributional impacts of structural adjustment on poor households.

name: SAA
key_interests: Airline revival post-business rescue, Takatso consortium deal, route profitability. Peripheral to the five core reform packages; serves as cautionary tale on SOE restructuring.
